_note: Illustrative curated snapshot for a prototype.
chunks: The process: (1) order an at-home impression kit, (2) take your dental impressions and send them back, (3) a dental professional reviews your case and builds a 3D treatment plan, (4) approve your preview, and (5) receive your custom clear aligners.
Day aligners are worn 20–22 hours per day and are typically changed to the next step on the schedule in your plan. Night aligners are worn only while you sleep and usually take longer overall.
Mild soreness or pressure for the first few days of a new step is normal and expected — it means the aligners are working. This usually eases within 2–3 days as your teeth adjust.
Wearing schedule: For best results, keep aligners in except when eating, drinking anything other than water, or cleaning your teeth. Remove them by the back edges to avoid bending the trays.
Treatment length: Most plans run 4–10 months depending on your case. Your specific timeline is shown in your approved treatment plan.
Aligner care: Rinse aligners in lukewarm (never hot) water and brush them gently. Hot water can warp the trays and affect fit.
